title: TLS, SSE-KMS, Bucket Keys, and Key Operations · Stakeholder TypeScript Mission
summary: A multi-institution health-research consortium. They need immutable consent versions, governed retention, archive retrieval, least-privilege access points, SSE-KMS, key rotation, legal-hold evidence, independent backup, and timed restore defense. Planned artifact: Deliver Research Consent Record Vault with acceptance evidence, rubric defense, reflection, and a changed-constraint extension. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and eviden…
objectives: In a strict Node 24 and TypeScript 7 service, apply governance, compliance, legal hold, retention date, and authorized bypass by version with irreversible-risk review.
In a strict Node 24 and TypeScript 7 service, separate WORM retention, version recovery, replication, independent backup, restore testing, and account-compromise boundaries.
In a strict Node 24 and TypeScript 7 service, request, monitor, authorize, and communicate temporary archive restoration with tier, expiry, duplicate request, and failure states.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: S3, CloudFront, and TypeScript Commit to the mission model · Project Object Lock Modes
steps.instruction:
project case — A project TypeScript file-delivery team handles case 8993 during a Node stream backpressure incident. Build deterministic TypeScript evidence to separate TLS, server encryption, KMS policy, context, and rotation evidence; separate deterministic browser evidence from controlled Node and AWS transfer gates; then replace one typed fixture with unknown runtime input and prove rejection before AWS use. Browser code uses pure values and state transitions only. Node and AWS SDK packages, networks, S3, CloudFront, KMS, IAM, WAF, PostgreSQL, credentials, host state, handle and stream checks, load, faults, restore, billing, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. Competency probe: In a strict Node 24 and TypeScript 7 service, apply governance, compliance, legal hold, retention date, and authorized bypass by version with irreversible-risk review.

What must be established before implementation?

Evidence target: S3, CloudFront, and TypeScript Commit to the mission model · Project Object Lock Modes.
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for in a strict node 24 and typescript 7 service, apply governance, compliance, legal hold, retention date, and authorized bypass by version with irreversible-risk review. For S3, CloudFront, and TypeScript Commit to the mission model · Project Object Lock Modes, keep Object Lock Modes connected to executable TypeScript storage and delivery decision evidence.
